allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking helps circle-of-control decompose constraints into assumptions and testable actions instead of treating the whole situation as one helpless block.
allies.source_quote: Circle of Control works well with First Principles Thinking, where constraints are decomposed into controllable assumptions and testable actions.

allies.rationale_text: Risk-vs-uncertainty helps circle-of-control distinguish what should be hedged from what can actually be changed.
allies.source_quote: It also complements Risk vs. Uncertainty by distinguishing what should be hedged versus what can be actively changed.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Circle-of-control conflicts with systems-thinking when local optimization inside the control circle obscures broader feedback loops.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Potential tension appears with Systems Thinking when local optimization inside the control circle obscures broader feedback loops.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Circle-of-control conflicts with game-theory-payoffs when opponents can shift what is influenceable faster than the current control map assumes.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Another tension appears with Game Theory when opponents can shift your controllable set dynamically.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is concise and strongest on two named complements and two named tensions, so the file preserves that bounded surface without inventing extra antagonists.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation sprawl where the raw file itself keeps the relational surface tight and explanatory.
open_questions: The source does not provide a formal structured tension block, so the tension items remain normalized from the latticework discussion.
